page: Amazon Web Services c8id.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with an x86_64 architecture. It features 4 vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with hyper-threading, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of a 237 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and the network interface delivers a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-threaded performance. The instance achieves top-tier results in Geekbench single-core scoring and single-threaded bzip3 decompression, alongside strong performance in single-threaded gzip compression. However, multi-core scaling is a notable tradeoff; stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression fall into the lower percentiles due to the physical core count. Memory bandwidth for large blocks and database operations, including Redis SET performance, remain in the average tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c8id.xlarge offers a dense compute profile with high single-core efficiency and fast local NVMe storage, making it suitable for workloads that do not require massive parallelization. It is well-suited for single-threaded processing tasks, lightweight web serving, software compilation, and database operations that benefit from fast local storage and high clock speeds. It is less optimal for memory-intensive applications or large-scale multi-threaded parallel processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c8id.xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz with 4 vCPUs across 2 physical cores. It includes 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz and a bundled 237 GB local NVMe SSD. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core capabilities, particularly in Geekbench single-core tests and single-threaded bzip3 decompression, though multi-core scaling is limited by the physical core count. This hardware configuration provides a cost-effective balance for single-threaded tasks, software compilation, and database operations requiring fast local storage. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, text processing, and applications requiring high clock speeds without massive parallelization.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor, 8 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 237 GB local NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 4 vCPUs, 8 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 237 GB local NVMe SSD on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A compute-optimized instance pairing high-frequency single-core performance with fast local NVMe storage for single-threaded and database workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz with 4 vCPUs
8.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
Bundled 237 GB local NVMe SSD instance storage
Top-tier single-core and single-threaded decompression performance
Ideal for single-threaded tasks, text processing, and lightweight databases
